calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden for Paul Peterson.

Ms. McFadden spoke of her efforts in researching criminal cases and locating any of the Decedent's Client's who may have been entitled to refunds. Ms. McFadden also stated she would be comfortable with holding a reserve, that the reserve will not escheat to the State and that the Trust Account will escheat three years from the Decedent's date of death which was in 2015. In addition, Ms. McFadden stated that she received this information from the State Bar Ethics and Robert Henderson with the Chief Trial Counsel, and that the State Bar did not cite any rule regarding the three year period. The Court stated that it is inclined to grant the petition, but subject to review and finalizing the order. Ms. McFadden is to file something that proposes an outer time limit for retention of the Trust Account by May 17, 2017 and provide a revised order. The Second Amended First and Final Waiver of Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Continued To May-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-29-2017 Continued To Apr-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden for Paul Peterson.

The Court stated that the obstacle here is the money that is sitting in the Decedent's Attorney Trust Account and whether a Practice Administrator is needed. Ms. McFadden stated that it is her understanding that the Attorney Trust fund does not belong to the Estate, that there doesn't seem to be anything pending, that she has gone round and round with the Legal Ethics Hot Line and the Office of Chief Trial Counsel, and that they only talked about the Attorney Trust Account. Ms. McFadden also stated that she believes it is the Office of Trial Counsel's responsibility to get a Practice Administrator, and that the idea is to leave the Trust Account alone and if not claimed, it will escheat to the State. The Court questions due diligence with respect to tracking down the Decedent's Clients and whether this is the right way to proceed to find if any Clients are due anything. The Court responded to Ms. McFadden's request for a preliminary distribution by stating that it does not know what extent of preliminary distribution is appropriate. The Amended First and Final on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to April 26, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a fuller report on the Executor's obligation on the two issues raised above and what the Court's responsibility is on the Trust Account and the rights and return of files to the Clients.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Continued To Mar-29-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney to appear on February 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why a petition for final distribution or a report of status of administration has not been filed. Status re closing of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Chu. Not reported.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
